raw_consumer_statement: Per my credit card agreement, I am seeking to reopen my dispute at American Express Company, which refuses to correct a billing dispute on my bank statement. I formally request from the credit card issuer, merchant, and merchants bank the business opportunity disclosures required by FTC Federal Trade Commission, Rule 16 C.F.R. Part 437 & 15 U.S.C. 41-58. and the Earnings claim statement required by law. The Commission has extensive law enforcement experience challenging misleading earnings claims under section 5 of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. 45. I was never issued an earnings claim statment nor a business opportunity disclosure. This begs the question of the legitimacy of the merchant and the reputation of the merchant 's bank not knowing their customer 's business and not proactively protecting the market from financial crimes and reducing the risk of fraud with proper screening and diligence. Per the Internet Crime Complaint Center, I request a recall and a hold harmless letter, and all evidence and internal notes used to decide the outcome of the previous dispute from American Express Company.
